title: Fix false ASSIGNIN on interface input port connections
body:
## Summary

Follow-up to #7322. That PR suppressed false `ASSIGNIN` for continuous
assignments to net-type interface input ports (`assign sif.port = val`),
but missed the port-connection path (`.output_port(sif.port)`). 
Found in CVV integration: https://github.com/openhwgroup/core-v-verif/pull/2724

At `V3Width` time, port connections are still `AstPin` nodes — `V3Inst`
converts them to `AssignW` later. The existing check only matched
`AssignW` as parent, so port connections were not suppressed.

## Reproducer

```systemverilog
interface ifc_status (input wire busy);
endinterface

module dut (output logic sleep_o);
  initial sleep_o = 1'b1;
endmodule

module dut_wrap (ifc_status sif);
  dut u_dut (
    .sleep_o(sif.busy)  // Legal but triggers ASSIGNIN
  );
endmodule
```

On master:
```
%Error-ASSIGNIN: Assigning to input/const variable: 'busy'
```

## Changes

- `V3Width.cpp`: Extend ASSIGNIN suppression to also accept `AstPin` as parent node, since a port connection to a net-type input is semantically equivalent to a continuous assignment (IEEE 1800-2023 §23.3.3.3)

## Test

- Updated `test_regress/t/t_interface_input_port_assign.v` -- added port-connection scenario (Scenario 4) alongside existing continuous-assignment scenarios

---
Developed by PlanV GmbH, assisted with Claude Code.

Reviewed by YilouWang.
